item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article could not be sourced, and is assumed to be non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Air Pod prototypes? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's definitely not Photoshopped.

Because I absolutely could not afford Photoshop in 2012. This is some free downloaded app stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i literally never noticed that until now wtf

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My one complaint for the article that anyone could fix is a way better image for this article. This is clearly photoshopped even before you expand it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but feels kinda incomplete. A series 2 log would be nice. +1 for now.

item.title: Re: Question:
item.content:encoded: I know it's been like…years, but I'd love to see a second set of logs. I'd really like to see someone pushing it to see how long someone can wear it before they just go insane.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…new researchers are cautioned to increase the time of exposure only incrementally[…].

Increase incrementally, of course. I assume here that you meant 'little by little' or something along the lines.

For some reason I don't like this article but, hey, it is at +57, so I must be in a minority.

item.title: Question:
item.content:encoded: This was my first SCP, and in looking back over it after recent comments, I definitely think it needs a bit of cleanup. The thing I'm uncertain about is whether I should leave the Test Logs as is— or continue with series 2. Opinions appreciated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, thanks! I appreciate it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've read most of your articles now, and I liked them all. I don't know what to say other than keep doing whatever you're doing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens when you give it to someone with synesthesia?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted, although begrudgingly. I was searching the archives for a headphone related SCP, because I was considering writing one, and found yours. And realised that's probably better than what I would have written.

Kudos, you bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Subject eventually reported: “I think it was my cat… it sounded just like my cat. He was singing a song to me. About fish.”

I upvoted here. Looks like I'm not the only one.

This could maybe use a touch more cleanup, but I really like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Late chiming in, but multiple articles on the site directly contradict each other as to whether Class A amnesiacs are the strongest or weakest amnesiac (or presumably, somewhere in between). So yeah. Definitely no canon there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this is the biggest flaw.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that does sound more clinical… but now it's going to bug me until I can think of the right one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think "surreal" is the word you are looking for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Singing cats, and a string quartet with the instruments replaced by Confucianism and a small spaniel?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This music ranges from mundane to the seemingly paranormal in nature

Do you mean "The effects of this music ranges …"? Because I have no idea what "paranormal music" would sound like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh, that is a cool thought. I'm going to try to finish the second series of logs, today… because that death you're mentioning is the only one that occurs, and I think I might have phrased the details badly. No deaths are directly caused by this SCP, or by any resulting crazy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the descriptions of what people heard. I suppose to be a complete SCP it needs the dangerous side effects, but it would be nice to be something other than "go crazy and die". Maybe with an individual its no big deal, but if 3 people that have listened get together, they sing in harmony and then something really fucked up happens.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is actually a pretty nice combination of creepy and fun. I mean, it's fun — it could be one of those where you have hundreds of logs submitted by other people — but not too fun. There's a consequence in using it, in other words, and chance that you'll be faced with that consequence whether you want to or not. Plus, music from rocks that changes depending on who's listening to it is nicely creepy by itself.

Structural quibble: "demonstrates an influence as often psychoactive as it is auditory." That's kind of poetic for a lab report. At the very least, moving 'often' in front of 'demonstrates' would make the sentence more structurally sound.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My first thought was of playing the brown note.

…But then, it wouldn't have been subtle at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, for some reason I'd always thought of A as the most powerful… probably because of SCP-231.

But to the headcanon point: I think yours is very reasonable. The personal headcanon I'd offer is that the Foundation has some of the same problems that any large human organization with a lot of people in a lot of places has to deal with. As deadly important as their mission is, bureaucratic isolation must be an issue, sometimes.

For this particular item, I imagined it being stored at Sector-28 or a similar location… some facility that deals exclusively with testing/storing Safe objects (especially for potential marketing), staffed with a lot of entry-level researchers. It's one thing to be instructed on memetics etc. and another to have the fear of god put in you by experience.

… I've also been playing with showing a learning-curve in the research team, in the logs. Suspension of disbelief is pretty vital, so if I write anyone doing something that crosses the believable/dumb line, I'd appreciate someone calling me out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Added one round of additions, more coming…. pending my ability to distinguish between "interesting" and "really dumb".

I can agree the concept is really simplistic (to the point of blandess, without logs), but that's also the reason this didn't fall into draft-doldrums. The two kinds of SCP entries I enjoy most are 1) the ones that horrify you to your bones, by perfectly playing on primal parano fears, or 2) the ones that are just fun/immersive to read, and give you a big chunk of reports, interviews, etc. The second kind seem to do a little more with the whole Foundation 'Verse conceit, and sometimes play up the personalities of the people involved. The best read a little more like science fiction, which I rend to prefer to creepypasta, anyway.

Er. All that to say, I suspect I'm better with bizarre than horrifying, and was trying to play to existing strengths.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That works better, yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
yeeeah, when you noted this I went back over it— and it kinda undermines the whole "research file" concept, not to have it. I was so worried about the description being overlong and bland that I overcompensated with the vagueness. Edited, now.

…and probably ten more times.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yeah, fixed that in my draft after delta_vee's comment… will update the edit when I get it to sound right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…staff members who have been assigned to SCP-231 are allowed to take a Class A Amnesiac before leaving the project…

…Should any personnel begin displaying obsession with SCP-140 or signs of possible memetic contamination, they are to be issued a Class A Amnesiac…

…reassigning personnel after a ██-month stay at Outpost Delta, applying Class A amnesiacs, and returning them to work on 270-1 at the end of a ██-month period…

…Class-A (short-term memory) amnesiacs are to be considered an antidote for the extremely recently exposed… …Class-B (long-term memory) and Class-C (total wipe) amnesiacs are of moderate effectiveness in "curing" an infected individual…

The strongest amnesiac is the one that causes total memory and personality wipe - you can't get any stronger than that. First three quotes clearly indicate that Class A only removes selective chunks of memory, no more than several month long.

But, again, There Is No Canon.

Still, I wouldn't say that there is a general consensus on this stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Besides, the general consensus is that Class A is the most powerful amnesiac, with B and C being the least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
<head-canon> After that epic fuck up with 884, Foundation declassified its file for all personnel. From that day on, all new recruits were required to read it, and learn from mistakes of those who came before them. And if any researcher or agent, anywhere, ever, requested to test an SCP object on himself, he would be dragged for a psych exam, and if it would turn out that he wasn't going crazy, suffering from some meme, or tripping on acid; if it would turn out that he is simply just that stupid, he would be dosed with class C and dumped on some roadside in Nowhere, Middle of. </head-canon>

But that doesn't really affect my vote.

The concept is a little bland. I'm inclined to upvote, but I'll wait and see if you add anything more interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …Okay, the description doesn't say what they do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Synesthesia! +1 for that alone, the rest is bonus-goodness!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I smiled at the bit about the cat singing. Also, the phrase "it was brown" is both funny and horrifying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do like this, Cellar, but the lack of effect description (even in overview form) in the actual description section bugs me, ever so slightly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
thanks!

I mentioned it in the announcement thread, but not here— this was kind of a self-challenge 3-hour-limit SCP. I'm still working on more test logs, but I want to be selective about which ones I put in.

I'm updating it again later, there's a log with deaf subject I'd like to finish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An upvote from me; it was a very intriguing idea that I think was executed very well.
Just one question though… where are the other logs? There is mention of a Log 01, 02, and 03, but there is only pieces of Logs 01 and 02…
Very good anyway!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1) "It was beige"
"I know it was, honey, but that wasn't the *name* on the paint chip."

2) "It was tan."
"In that orange, New Jersey coastal kind of way."

3) "It was khaki."
"And don't get me started on the animal prints."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Beige? Tan? Khaki?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
you know what. "Maroon" sounded off, so I randomly picked another dull color.

and then I was like… "…oh wait, someone will probably think that is a subtle poo joke."

and then I was like "…so be it."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …did you really just slide in a subtle poop joke?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a really, really well written safe object. Enthusiastically upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
D-███: After the first two minutes, the subject was seen to raise both hands and press them over the earpieces. The subject's description of the [REDACTED].

whyyyyy

upvote though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually really like this. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh, thanks. I'm not really quite happy with the logs+ breadth of the experiments, yet… but I'm glad someone liked that detail.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not bad.
What got me was the singing cat.. strong recollections of Monday begins on Saturday

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … does that mean I've missed a dozen instances of other, better headphone SCPs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First time here?
